LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. EVEN ASSUMING THE FACTS CONTAINED IN THE COMPLAINT ARE TRUE, THE PLAINTIFF CANNOT STATE A LEGAL CLAIM FOR LIABILITY FOR TRESPASS TO CHATTELS. EVEN WHEN CONSTRUED LIBERALLY, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE DEFENDANTS ACTED UNLAWFULLY WHEN IT TOWED PLAINTIFF'S CAR. PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF 1001 OF THE TRANSPORTATION CODE IS AN IMAGINATIVE BUT UNREASONABLE ONE AND WOULD LEAD TO ABSURD RESULTS. MOREOVER, PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS OF MISLEADING REPRESENTATION AND ERRATIC ENFORCEMENT ARE NOT BASED ON ANY ESTABLISHED LAW AND DO NOT PROVIDE A SUFFICIENT BASIS TO SUPPORT A FINDING OF "UNLAWFUL INTERFERENCE" NECESSARY TO A PROPER TRESPASS TO CHATTELS CAUSE OF ACTION. FINALLY, THERE DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE ANY REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT THE PLAINTIFF CAN CURE THE DEFECTS IN THE PLEADING. GOODMAN V. KENNEDY (1976) 18 CAL.APP.3RD.335, 384. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-07-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Deborah C. England, Esq. 434-9800 for pltf & opposing party; Vince Chhabria, Deputy City Atty 554-4674 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's Demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Even assuming the facts contained in the complaint are true, the Plaintiff cannot state a legal claim for liability for Trespass to Chattels. Even when construed liberally, Plaintiff has failed to demonstrate that the Defendants acted unlawfully when it towed Plaintiff's car. Plaintiff's interpretation of 1001 of the Transportation Code is an imaginative but unreasonable one and would lead to absurd results. Moreover, Plaintiff's allegations of misleading representation and erratic enforcement are not based on any established law and do not provide a sufficient basis to support a finding of "unlawful interference" necessary to a proper trespass to chattels cause of action. Finally, there does not appear to be any reasonable probability that the Plaintiff can cure the defects in the pleading. Goodman v. Kennedy (1976) 18 Cal.App.3rd.335, 384. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-24-2010 Off Calendar
